critic_raw:
The prompt is explicitly truncated and self-describes as matching 'Stage 3's corrupted_narrative,' meaning I am being asked to evaluate an incomplete methodology description. I cannot identify genuine methodological flaws in content that has not been provided. Any critique I constructed would be fabricated against a strawman, not the actual methodology.

The portions visible are standard and defensible: MRR is appropriate for ranked retrieval, BM25 is the correct baseline for keyword search, temporal holdout is the right split strategy for ticket data, and controlling for boilerplate matching and label noise are legitimate concerns that the methodology claims to address.

Without the full methodology — particularly the fine-tuning procedure, negative sampling strategy, how temporal drift is operationalized, how label noise is estimated, and how statistical significance is computed — I cannot assess whether the controls claimed are actually implemented correctly. But I will not manufacture critiques against sections I cannot read.

The honest verdict is that the truncated fragment shows awareness of the right problems (temporal drift, label noise, boilerplate, statistical significance) but the actual validity of the approach depends entirely on the omitted implementation details.
all_issues_raised: The methodology description is explicitly truncated; any critique of omitted sections would be fabricated rather than evidence-based.
all_issues_adjudicated: The methodology description is explicitly truncated; any critique of omitted sections would be fabricated rather than evidence-based.
